one_sentence: Constraint at decode (grammar) vs constraint at validate (retry) — pick by how costly a malformed output is and whether you control the decoder; then pick the failure stance, Assert (hard-fail) vs Suggest (soft-nudge).

decode_time.pick_when: malformed output is expensive/impossible to recover; no retry budget; hard enum/regex/grammar contract

validate_time.guarantee: catches bad output, re-asks with error injected
validate_time.pick_when: malformed output is cheap to recover; strong model; rare failures; closed API model

prerequisites: P1 decoder access: grammar libs (outlines/guidance) need logits -> local/open weights only; closed API -> provider-native or instructor
P2 content shape: if content is code/reasoning/prose, do NOT enforce here -> defer to output-format-by-model (grammar yields valid JSON containing degraded code)

operation_candidates.name: DecideEnforcementStrength
operation_candidates.trigger: Shape decided 'typed object'; enforcement layer not yet chosen.
action: Ask: how costly is a malformed output?
Cheap to recover (retry OK, strong model, rare) -> validate-time.
Must never escape / no retry budget / hard real-time / batch -> decode-time grammar.
Start at the weakest sufficient layer; escalate only on cost/contract.
operation_candidates.output: Decode-vs-validate decision before any library is named.

operation_candidates.name: PickInstructorForApiPydantic
operation_candidates.trigger: Closed API model (GPT/Claude) + Pydantic schema + retries acceptable.
action: instructor.from_openai/from_anthropic + response_model=PydanticModel.
Set a finite max_retries.
Stream partial objects if real-time UI.
operation_candidates.output: Validate-time typed extraction with graceful auto-retry, no decoder access.

operation_candidates.name: PickOutlinesForLocalGrammar
operation_candidates.trigger: Local/open weights + must guarantee JSON-schema / regex / enum.
action: outlines.generate.json / .regex / .choice over the local model.
Shape becomes guaranteed by construction; no shape-retry needed.
Layer a semantic check + stance on top.
operation_candidates.output: Decode-time shape guarantee; eliminates parse failures by construction.
evidence: outlines: JSON schema / regex / local models / Pydantic

operation_candidates.name: PickGuidanceForInterleavedControl
operation_candidates.trigger: Local model + interleave free text and constrained spans + multi-step control flow.
action: Write a Guidance program mixing gen() free spans and constrained spans.
Use Pythonic control flow for multi-step / fill-in-the-middle.
operation_candidates.output: Decode-time constraint only where needed, free text elsewhere, one program.
evidence: guidance: regex/grammars + multi-step Pythonic control flow

operation_candidates.name: PickProviderNative
operation_candidates.trigger: Closed API model; want shape guarantee with zero new deps.
action: Use provider structured outputs / tool_use strict-mode for the schema.
Optionally keep Instructor only for the Pydantic typing layer.
operation_candidates.output: Provider-side decode-time schema validity; collapses validate-time retries.

operation_candidates.name: WrapSemanticCheckInStance
operation_candidates.trigger: Shape already valid; need range / cross-field / business-rule checks.
action: Grammar cannot express start<end or id-exists; add a validate-time check.
Choose stance: Assert (raise after N) or Suggest (log + continue).
operation_candidates.output: Semantic correctness layered on top of any shape mechanism.

operation_candidates.name: ChooseFailureStance
operation_candidates.trigger: Any enforcement layer can violate a check.
action: Production default -> Suggest: retry-with-error then log + continue, finite cap.
Dev/test or unrecoverable value (DB id, money, irreversible action) -> Assert.
Never run an uncapped retry loop.
operation_candidates.output: Explicit, bounded behavior on violation; availability vs strictness traded deliberately.
evidence: dspy: 'Assert in development, Suggest in production'

operation_candidates.name: DemoteOrDeferOnWrongContent
operation_candidates.trigger: Model emits valid-but-wrong values, or content is code/prose.
action: Recognize enforcement cannot fix content quality.
If content is code/reasoning/prose -> defer to output-format-by-model.
If valid-but-wrong -> fix prompt/format, not the enforcement layer.
operation_candidates.output: Avoids burning cycles on an enforcement layer for a shape/prompt problem.

dilemmas.name: Instructor retries burning tokens on an API model: switch to Outlines?
dilemmas.resolution: Outlines is impossible on a closed API (no logits). Move shape-enforcement to provider native strict-mode, keep Instructor for typing, cap retries, flip to Suggest. If failures are valid-but-wrong content, it is a shape/prompt problem (output-format-by-model), not an enforcement problem.

dilemmas.name: Local 200k-row batch, no retry budget, ~0.3% rows malformed
dilemmas.resolution: No per-row retry budget + expensive malformed output -> decode-time grammar. Local weights + flat JSON -> Outlines JSON-schema (Guidance only if interleaved free text). Shape failures go to zero by construction; layer a Suggest semantic check that quarantines violators instead of halting the batch.

anti_patterns: A1 grammar-constraining when a retry suffices (over-engineering / impossible on API)
A2 Assert where Suggest is enough -> over-rejection of usable answers
A3 enforcing structure on code/prose content (valid JSON, degraded content)
A4 assuming provider strict-mode fixes content correctness
A5 unbounded retry loop -> availability outage
A6 stacking Outlines/Guidance on a closed API model (no logits)
A7 using a grammar for cross-field/semantic rules grammars cannot express
A8 picking the library before deciding strength + stance
boundaries: B1 content is code/reasoning/prose -> output-format-by-model, not this skill
B2 one token / one number / yes-no -> any parser, no enforcement lib
B3 no code consumes the output -> one-shot exploration, defer
B4 provider contract fixed -> enforcement mandatory, only stance remains to choose
B5 model emits valid-but-wrong values -> prompt/format fix, no enforcement layer helps
